title: SQL Skill Check
tagline: Real company-tagged SQL questions — self-rated against the model answer

questions.prompt:
Average Order Value

Schema:
```
orders(order_id, customer_id, order_total, order_date, status, amount)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer: SELECT AVG(order_total) AS avg_order_value FROM orders;

questions.prompt:
Find Cheapest Product

Schema:
```
products(product_id, name, price, category, sku, manufacturer)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer: SELECT product_id, name, price FROM products ORDER BY price ASC LIMIT 1;

questions.prompt:
New vs. Returning Customer Report

Schema:
```
orders(order_id, customer_id, order_date, status, amount)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT
  CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 1 THEN 'New' ELSE 'Returning' END AS customer_type,
  COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS customers
FROM (SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) t
GROUP BY CASE WHEN cnt = 1 THEN 'New' ELSE 'Returning' END;

questions.prompt:
Join Three Tables

Schema:
```
orders(customer_id, order_id, order_date, status, amount), customers(customer_id, name, email, signup_date, country, city), products(name, product_id, category, price, sku, manufacturer), order_items(order_id, product_id)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT o.order_id, c.name AS customer, p.name AS product
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id;

questions.prompt:
Pivot Daily Sales

Schema:
```
sales(sale_date, amount)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT sale_date,
  SUM(CASE WHEN amount > 0 THEN amount END) AS positive_sales,
  COUNT(*) AS txn_count
FROM sales GROUP BY sale_date ORDER BY sale_date;

Use COALESCE for Null Handling

Schema:
```
employees(emp_id, name, bonus, email, hire_date, salary, dept_id, manager_id, job_title)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer: SELECT emp_id, name, COALESCE(bonus, 0) AS bonus FROM employees;

questions.prompt:
Self-Join for Hierarchical Data

Schema:
```
employees(emp_id, name, manager_id, hierarchy, level, email, hire_date, salary, dept_id, job_title)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH RECURSIVE hierarchy AS (
  SELECT emp_id, name, manager_id, 1 AS level FROM employees WHERE manager_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT e.emp_id, e.name, e.manager_id, h.level+1
  FROM employees e JOIN hierarchy h ON e.manager_id = h.emp_id)
SELECT * FROM hierarchy ORDER BY level, emp_id;

questions.prompt:
Advanced JOIN with Three Tables

Schema:
```
customers(customer_id, name, order_id, product_id, qty, email, signup_date, country, city), orders(customer_id, order_id, name, product_id, qty, order_date, status, amount), products(name, product_id, customer_id, order_id, qty, category, price, sku, manufacturer), order_items(order_id, product_id, qty, customer_id, name)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT c.name, o.order_id, p.name AS product, oi.qty
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id;

questions.prompt:
Overlapping Shift Detector

Schema:
```
shifts(shift_id, employee_id, start_time, end_time)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS shifts;
CREATE TABLE shifts (
  shift_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  employee_id INT,
  start_time TIMESTAMP,
  end_time TIMESTAMP
);
INSERT INTO shifts VALUES
  (1, 100, '2024-05-01 08:00', '2024-05-01 12:00'),
  (2, 100, '2024-05-01 11:00', '2024-05-01 15:00'),
  (3, 100, '2024-05-01 16:00', '2024-05-01 20:00'),
  (4, 200, '2024-05-02 09:00', '2024-05-02 17:00'),
  (5, 200, '2024-05-02 13:00', '2024-05-02 18:00'),
  (6, 300, '2024-05-03 06:00', '2024-05-03 10:00');

SELECT a.shift_id, b.shift_id
FROM shifts a JOIN shifts b
  ON a.employee_id = b.employee_id AND a.shift_id < b.shift_id
 AND a.start_time < b.end_time AND b.start_time < a.end_time;

questions.prompt:
Self-Join to Identify Missing Supervisors

Schema:
```
employees(emp_id, name, supervisor_id, email, hire_date, salary, dept_id, manager_id, job_title)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS employees;
CREATE TABLE employees (
  emp_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT,
  supervisor_id INT
);
INSERT INTO employees (emp_id, name, supervisor_id) VALUES
  (1, 'Alice', NULL),
  (2, 'Bob', 1),
  (3, 'Carol', 1),
  (4, 'Dan', 99),
  (5, 'Eve', 2),
  (6, 'Frank', 88),
  (7, 'Grace', 3),
  (8, 'Hank', 77);

SELECT e.emp_id, e.name FROM employees e
LEFT JOIN employees s ON e.supervisor_id = s.emp_id
WHERE e.supervisor_id IS NOT NULL AND s.emp_id IS NULL;

questions.prompt:
Transaction Anomaly Detector

Schema:
```
transactions(txn_id, amount, transaction_id, customer_id, transaction_time, txn_type, status)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH stats AS (
  SELECT AVG(amount) AS mu, AVG(amount*amount) - AVG(amount)*AVG(amount) AS variance
  FROM transactions
)
SELECT t.txn_id, t.amount
FROM transactions t, stats s
WHERE s.variance > 0 AND ABS(t.amount - s.mu) > 3 * SQRT(s.variance);

WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT job_title, salary,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY job_title ORDER BY salary) AS rn,
    COUNT(*)    OVER (PARTITION BY job_title)                  AS n

questions.prompt:
Employee Salary Ranking Analysis

Given employees(emp_id, dept_id, salary, ...) and departments(dept_id, department_name, ...), rank every employee within their department by salary DESC using DENSE_RANK so ties collapse to one rank. Return emp_id, dept_id, salary, and the dense_rank. The enrichment walks through how to extend this for 'second-highest per department' (Probe 1), 'highest AND second-highest side by side' (Probe 1b), and a rotated pivot with departments as columns (Probe 1c) — including the single-person and empty-department edge cases that motivate the DENSE_RANK + LEFT JOIN pattern.

Schema:
```
departments(dept_id, department_name, location, manager_id), employees(emp_id, name, email, hire_date, salary, dept_id, manager_id, job_title)
```
questions.model_answer:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS employees;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS departments;
CREATE TABLE departments (
    dept_id          INT PRIMARY KEY,
    department_name  TEXT NOT NULL,
    location         TEXT,
    manager_id       INT
);
CREATE TABLE employees (
    emp_id      INT PRIMARY KEY,
    name        TEXT NOT NULL,
    email       TEXT,
    hire_date   DATE,
    salary      NUMERIC NOT NULL,
    dept_id     INT REFERENCES departments,
    manager_id  INT,
    job_title   TEXT
);

-- Departments — manager_id points to the dept head (NULL for empty depts).
-- The dept head is the employee whose own manager_id is NULL in that dept.
INSERT INTO departments (dept_id, department_name, location, manager_id) VALUES
    (1, 'Engineering',     'Mumbai',    1),    -- Alex heads Engineering
    (2, 'Sales',           'New York',  6),    -- Finn heads Sales
    (3, 'Marketing',       'San Jose',  9),    -- Indy heads Marketing (sole employee)
    (4, 'Human Resources', 'Bangalore', NULL), -- empty dept, no manager
    (5, 'Operations',      'Singapore', 10);   -- Jamie heads Operations

-- Employees — manager_id points to the dept head; the dept head themselves has manager_id=NULL.
-- Ties at the top (Alex/Bailey both at $210K) — only Alex is the manager;
-- ties in salary don't imply ties in role.
INSERT INTO employees (emp_id, name, hire_date, salary, dept_id, manager_id, job_title) VALUES
    (1, 'Alex',    '2020-01-10', 210000, 1, NULL, 'Engineering Director'),
    (2, 'Bailey',  '2020-03-15', 210000, 1, 1,    'Principal Engineer'),
    (3, 'Casey',   '2021-06-01', 180000, 1, 1,    'Senior Engineer'),
    (4, 'Drew',    '2021-09-12', 180000, 1, 1,    'Senior Engineer'),
    (5, 'Ellis',   '2022-04-20', 155000, 1, 1,    'Engineer'),
    (6, 'Finn',    '2019-11-05', 240000, 2, NULL, 'Sales Director'),
    (7, 'Gray',    '2020-07-22', 190000, 2, 6,    'Senior AE'),
    (8, 'Harper',  '2022-02-14', 135000, 2, 6,    'SDR'),
    (9, 'Indy',    '2021-12-01', 155000, 3, NULL, 'Head of Marketing'),
    -- HR (dept 4) has no employees — the empty-dept edge case
    (10,'Jamie',   '2019-05-08', 175000, 5, NULL, 'Ops Director'),
    (11,'Kennedy', '2021-01-18', 140000, 5, 10,   'Ops Manager'),
    (12,'Lane',    '2022-08-30', 110000, 5, 10,   'Ops Analyst');

-- The headline query — per-department dense-rank by salary.
-- Edge cases the enrichment walks through:
--   ENG  — ties at top (Alex+Bailey at rk 1, Casey+Drew at rk 2)
--   MKTG — single employee, no "second-highest"
--   HR   — no employees at all
SELECT emp_id, dept_id, salary,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY dept_id ORDER BY salary DESC) AS rk
FROM employees
ORDER BY dept_id, rk, emp_id;

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.

Dynamic SQL Simulation Concept

A products catalog stores category-specific attributes as a JSON blob in `attribute_json`. Different categories have different keys:

  • phones — screen_size, ram_gb, storage_gb
  • books  — author, pages, isbn
  • shirts — size, color, fabric

Write a SQL query that returns each product with its category-relevant primary and secondary attributes EXTRACTED into named columns. This is the 'dynamic SQL simulation' pattern: in a real app you'd code-generate per-category SELECTs; in pure SQL you simulate the same effect with CASE + json_extract().

Expected output (six rows ordered by category, product_id):

  product_id | category | primary_attr      | secondary_attr
  3          | book     | Ursula K. Le Guin | 254
  4          | book     | N.K. Jemisin      | 449
  1          | phone    | 6.1               | 8
  2          | phone    | 6.7               | 12
  5          | shirt    | M                 | navy
  6          | shirt    | L                 | olive

Schema:
```
products(product_id, category, attribute_json, name, price, sku, manufacturer)
```
questions.model_answer:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS products;
CREATE TABLE products (
    product_id     INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    category       TEXT NOT NULL,
    attribute_json JSONB NOT NULL
);
INSERT INTO products VALUES
  (1, 'phone', '{"screen_size": 6.1, "ram_gb": 8,  "storage_gb": 128}'),
  (2, 'phone', '{"screen_size": 6.7, "ram_gb": 12, "storage_gb": 256}'),
  (3, 'book',  '{"author": "Ursula K. Le Guin", "pages": 254, "isbn": "9780441478125"}'),
  (4, 'book',  '{"author": "N.K. Jemisin",      "pages": 449, "isbn": "9780316229296"}'),
  (5, 'shirt', '{"size": "M", "color": "navy",  "fabric": "cotton"}'),
  (6, 'shirt', '{"size": "L", "color": "olive", "fabric": "cotton-blend"}');

SELECT
    product_id,
    category,
    CASE category
        WHEN 'phone' THEN attribute_json->>'screen_size'
        WHEN 'book'  THEN attribute_json->>'author'
        WHEN 'shirt' THEN attribute_json->>'size'
    END AS primary_attr,
    CASE category
        WHEN 'phone' THEN attribute_json->>'ram_gb'
        WHEN 'book'  THEN attribute_json->>'pages'
        WHEN 'shirt' THEN attribute_json->>'color'
    END AS secondary_attr
FROM products
ORDER BY category, product_id;

WITH d AS (
  SELECT dept_id, AVG(salary) AS mu, AVG(salary*salary) - AVG(salary)*AVG(salary) AS var_pop
  FROM employees GROUP BY dept_id
)
SELECT dept_id, mu AS avg_salary,
  CASE WHEN var_pop > 0 THEN ROUND(mu - SQRT(var_pop), 2) ELSE mu END AS lower_bound,

FROM d ORDER BY dept_id;

questions.prompt:
Merge Employee and Department Records

Schema:
```
employees(emp_id, name, dept_id, dept_name, email, hire_date, salary, manager_id, job_title)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
-- Setup — two source dim tables
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS departments CASCADE;
CREATE TABLE departments (dept_id INT PRIMARY KEY, dept_name TEXT NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO departments VALUES (1,'Engineering'),(2,'Sales'),(3,'Marketing'),(4,'Finance');

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS employees CASCADE;
CREATE TABLE employees (
  emp_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  name   TEXT,
  dept_id INT,
  dept_name TEXT
);
INSERT INTO employees VALUES
  (101,'Alice',1,NULL),
  (102,'Bob',  2,NULL),
  (103,'Carol',3,'Mktg'),     -- stale dept_name
  (104,'Dave', 9,NULL);       -- dept_id 9 doesn't exist in departments

-- ╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
-- │  Postgres pattern: UPDATE FROM (the MERGE-equivalent)    │
-- │  Sync employees.dept_name from departments.dept_name      │
-- │  whenever the dept_id matches.                            │
-- ╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
UPDATE employees e
   SET dept_name = d.dept_name
  FROM departments d
 WHERE e.dept_id = d.dept_id;

-- Show the result
SELECT emp_id, name, dept_id, dept_name FROM employees ORDER BY emp_id;

SELECT manager_id, COUNT(*) AS reports FROM chain GROUP BY manager_id;

Complex Aggregation and Join: Employee Project Assignments and Salary Calculation


questions.prompt:
Complex Data Warehouse Aggregation and Trend Analysis for Multi-Channel Sales Optimization

Schema:
```
sales(channel, sale_date, amount, monthly)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH monthly AS (
  SELECT channel, DATE_TRUNC('month', sale_date)::DATE AS mo, SUM(amount) AS total
  FROM sales GROUP BY channel, DATE_TRUNC('month', sale_date)::DATE)
SELECT channel, mo, total,
  AVG(total) OVER (PARTITION BY channel ORDER BY mo ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS rolling_3mo,

  COUNT(first_paid_date) AS converted_to_paid,

WITH counted AS (
  SELECT a.user_id, a.show_id, a.episode_id, a.started_at,
    COUNT(*) AS eps_in_last_24h
  FROM episode_plays a
  JOIN episode_plays b
    ON b.user_id = a.user_id
   AND b.show_id = a.show_id
   AND julianday(b.started_at) BETWEEN julianday(a.started_at) - 1 AND julianday(a.started_at)

WHERE eps_in_last_24h >= 2;

questions.prompt:
Rebuffering Rate by ISP — QoE Analysis

Schema:
```
qoe(session_id, isp, watched_seconds, rebuf_seconds, rebuf_count)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS qoe;
CREATE TABLE qoe (session_id INT, isp TEXT, watched_seconds INT, rebuf_seconds INT, rebuf_count INT);
INSERT INTO qoe (session_id, isp, watched_seconds, rebuf_seconds, rebuf_count) VALUES
  (1,'Comcast',1800,30,2),
  (2,'Comcast',2400,60,3),
  (3,'Comcast',1500,15,1),
  (4,'Verizon',3000,10,1),
  (5,'Verizon',2700,20,2),
  (6,'Verizon',1200,5,1),
  (7,'AT&T',2000,80,4),
  (8,'AT&T',1500,90,5),
  (9,'Spectrum',2500,25,1),
  (10,'Spectrum',1800,18,2);
SELECT isp,
  COUNT(*) AS sessions,
  ROUND(SUM(rebuf_seconds) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(watched_seconds + rebuf_seconds), 0), 3) AS rebuf_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(rebuf_count), 2) AS avg_rebufs_per_session
FROM qoe
GROUP BY isp
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
ORDER BY rebuf_pct DESC;

questions.prompt:
Concurrent-Stream Cap Violation per Account


Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH events AS (
  SELECT account_id, started_at AS t, +1 AS delta FROM streams
  UNION ALL
  SELECT account_id, ended_at,           -1        FROM streams
),
active AS (
  SELECT account_id, t,
    SUM(delta) OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY t ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) AS concurrent
  FROM events
)
SELECT DISTINCT account_id
FROM active
WHERE concurrent > 1;

Title Launch Curve — D7 vs D28 Watch Hours

  ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN (played_at - launched_on) <= INTERVAL '28 days' THEN watch_minutes END) / 60.0, 1) AS d28_hours,
  ROUND(100.0 *
    SUM(CASE WHEN (played_at - launched_on) <= INTERVAL '7 days'  THEN watch_minutes END) /
    NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN (played_at - launched_on) <= INTERVAL '28 days' THEN watch_minutes END), 0),

questions.prompt:
Search-then-Play Attribution Within Session

Schema:
```
searches(user_id, search_ts, query_text, top_title_id), plays(user_id, play_ts, title_id)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT s.query_text,
  COUNT(*) AS searches,
  COUNT(p.title_id) AS resulting_plays,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(p.title_id) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS search_to_play_pct
FROM searches s
LEFT JOIN plays p
  ON p.user_id  = s.user_id
 AND p.title_id = s.top_title_id
 AND EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (p.play_ts - s.search_ts)) / 86400.0 BETWEEN 0 AND 7
GROUP BY s.query_text
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1
ORDER BY search_to_play_pct DESC;

questions.prompt:
Cohort Retention by Signup Week — Week-N Active Rate

Schema:
```
users(user_id, signup_date, name, email, country), activity(user_id, activity_date)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH cohorts AS (
  SELECT user_id, DATE_TRUNC('week', signup_date::date)::date AS cohort_week
  FROM users
),
dedup AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT user_id, DATE_TRUNC('week', activity_date::date)::date AS active_week
  FROM activity
),
joined AS (
  SELECT c.cohort_week, d.active_week,
         ((d.active_week - c.cohort_week) / 7) AS week_n,
         c.user_id
  FROM cohorts c JOIN dedup d USING (user_id)
)
SELECT cohort_week, week_n,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS active_users,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) /
    FIRST_VALUE(COUNT(DISTINCT user_id)) OVER (PARTITION BY cohort_week ORDER BY week_n), 1) AS retention_pct
FROM joined
WHERE week_n BETWEEN 0 AND 12
GROUP BY cohort_week, week_n
ORDER BY cohort_week, week_n;

questions.prompt:
Profile-Level Watch Distribution per Account

  SUM(watch_minutes) AS profile_minutes,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(watch_minutes) /
    SUM(SUM(watch_minutes)) OVER (PARTITION BY account_id), 1) AS share_pct,
  RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY SUM(watch_minutes) DESC) AS profile_rank
FROM plays
GROUP BY account_id, profile_id
ORDER BY account_id, share_pct DESC;

Late-Arriving Dimensions — Inferred-Member Pattern


WITH current_content AS (
  SELECT content_id, title, genre,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY content_id
      ORDER BY COALESCE(effective_date, '9999-12-31') DESC
    ) AS rn
  FROM content_dim
)
SELECT v.view_id,
  COALESCE(c.title, 'Pending Metadata Update') AS title,

  ON v.content_id = c.content_id AND c.rn = 1;

questions.model_answer:
WITH daily_views AS (
  SELECT user_id, view_date
  FROM user_views
  GROUP BY user_id, view_date
),
islands AS (
  SELECT user_id, view_date,
    julianday(view_date) - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY view_date) AS island_group
  FROM daily_views
),
streak_counts AS (
  SELECT user_id,
    MIN(view_date) AS streak_start,
    MAX(view_date) AS streak_end,
    COUNT(*)       AS consecutive_days
  FROM islands
  GROUP BY user_id, island_group
)
SELECT *
FROM streak_counts
WHERE consecutive_days >= 2
ORDER BY consecutive_days DESC;

questions.prompt:
Content Gap — 30-Day Churntential Inactivity Window

Schema:
```
user_views(user_id, view_date)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH user_activity AS (
  SELECT user_id, view_date,
    LEAD(view_date) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY view_date) AS next_view_date
  FROM (SELECT DISTINCT user_id, view_date FROM user_views) t
)
SELECT user_id,
  view_date AS gap_start_date,
  next_view_date AS gap_end_date,
  (next_view_date::date - view_date::date) AS gap_duration
FROM user_activity
WHERE (next_view_date::date - view_date::date) > 5;

questions.model_answer:
WITH current_metadata AS (
  SELECT series_id, series_title, season_number
  FROM (
    SELECT *,
      ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY series_id ORDER BY effective_date DESC) AS rn
    FROM dim_series_metadata
  ) m
  WHERE rn = 1
),
daily_binges AS (
  SELECT v.user_id, v.series_id, v.view_date,
    COUNT(v.episode_id) AS episodes_watched,
    COALESCE(m.series_title, 'Series ID: ' || v.series_id || ' (Pending Metadata)') AS series_display_name
  FROM fact_viewing_history v
  LEFT JOIN current_metadata m USING (series_id)
  GROUP BY v.user_id, v.series_id, v.view_date, m.series_title
)
SELECT *
FROM daily_binges
WHERE episodes_watched >= 3
ORDER BY view_date DESC, episodes_watched DESC;

questions.prompt:
Driver Utilization Rate — Time-on-Trip / Time-Online

Schema:
```
driver_shifts(driver_id, online_at, offline_at), trips(driver_id, started_at, ended_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT s.driver_id,
  ROUND(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (s.offline_at - s.online_at)) / 86400.0) * 24.0, 2) AS hours_online,
  ROUND(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (t.ended_at - t.started_at)) / 86400.0) * 24.0, 2) AS hours_on_trip,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (t.ended_at - t.started_at)) / 86400.0)
              / NULLIF(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (s.offline_at - s.online_at)) / 86400.0), 0), 1) AS utilization_pct
FROM driver_shifts s
LEFT JOIN trips t ON t.driver_id = s.driver_id
GROUP BY s.driver_id
ORDER BY utilization_pct DESC;

questions.prompt:
Round-Trip Detection — Same-Day Return Rides

Schema:
```
rides(ride_id, rider_id, started_at, origin_zone, dest_zone)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS rides;
CREATE TABLE rides (
  ride_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  rider_id INT,
  started_at TIMESTAMP,
  origin_zone TEXT,
  dest_zone TEXT
);
INSERT INTO rides (ride_id, rider_id, started_at, origin_zone, dest_zone) VALUES
  (1, 10, '2025-03-01 08:00:00', 'A', 'B'),
  (2, 10, '2025-03-01 18:00:00', 'B', 'A'),
  (3, 11, '2025-03-02 09:00:00', 'C', 'D'),
  (4, 11, '2025-03-02 14:00:00', 'D', 'C'),
  (5, 12, '2025-03-03 07:00:00', 'E', 'F'),
  (6, 12, '2025-03-04 10:00:00', 'F', 'E'),
  (7, 13, '2025-03-05 06:00:00', 'G', 'H'),
  (8, 13, '2025-03-05 21:00:00', 'H', 'G'),
  (9, 14, '2025-03-06 12:00:00', 'I', 'J');

SELECT a.rider_id, a.ride_id AS first_ride, b.ride_id AS second_ride,
  a.origin_zone || ' -> ' || a.dest_zone AS first_leg,
  b.origin_zone || ' -> ' || b.dest_zone AS second_leg,
  CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (b.started_at - a.started_at)) / 86400.0) * 24 AS INTEGER) AS hours_between
FROM rides a
JOIN rides b
  ON b.rider_id = a.rider_id
 AND b.ride_id != a.ride_id
 AND (b.started_at) > (a.started_at)
 AND (b.started_at - a.started_at) <= INTERVAL '1 days'
ORDER BY a.rider_id, hours_between
LIMIT 20;


Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.

WITH pairs AS (
  SELECT a.user_id AS me, b.friend_id AS suggested_friend,
    COUNT(*) AS mutual_friends
  FROM friendships a
  JOIN friendships b
    ON a.friend_id = b.user_id
   AND a.user_id != b.friend_id
  GROUP BY a.user_id, b.friend_id
)

ORDER BY mutual_friends DESC, me, suggested_friend
LIMIT 20;

questions.prompt:
Group Engagement Pyramid — Posters / Commenters / Lurkers

Schema:
```
group_actions(group_id, user_id, action_type)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH per_user AS (
  SELECT group_id, user_id,
    SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'post'    THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS posts,
    SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'comment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS comments,
    SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'reaction' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS reactions
  FROM group_actions
  GROUP BY group_id, user_id
)
SELECT group_id,
  SUM(CASE WHEN posts >= 1                THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS posters,
  SUM(CASE WHEN posts = 0 AND comments >= 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS commenters,
  SUM(CASE WHEN posts = 0 AND comments = 0 AND reactions >= 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS reactors,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS active_members
FROM per_user
GROUP BY group_id
ORDER BY active_members DESC;

questions.prompt:
Comment Thread Depth — Recursive Reply Chain

Schema:
```
comments(comment_id, parent_id, post_id, posted_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH RECURSIVE chain AS (
  SELECT comment_id, parent_id, post_id, 1 AS depth
  FROM comments WHERE parent_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT c.comment_id, c.parent_id, c.post_id, ch.depth + 1
  FROM comments c JOIN chain ch ON c.parent_id = ch.comment_id
)
SELECT post_id,
  MAX(depth) AS max_depth,
  COUNT(*)   AS total_comments
FROM chain
GROUP BY post_id
ORDER BY max_depth DESC;

questions.prompt:
Reels Watch-Completion Buckets

Schema:
```
reel_plays(play_id, reel_id, watched_seconds, total_seconds)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH classified AS (
  SELECT *,
    CASE
      WHEN watched_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(total_seconds, 0) >= 0.95 THEN 'completed'
      WHEN watched_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(total_seconds, 0) >= 0.50 THEN 'engaged'
      WHEN watched_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(total_seconds, 0) >= 0.10 THEN 'sampled'
      ELSE 'bounced'
    END AS engagement_band
  FROM reel_plays
)
SELECT engagement_band,
  COUNT(*) AS plays,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 1) AS pct
FROM classified
GROUP BY engagement_band
ORDER BY plays DESC;

questions.prompt:


Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT conversation_id,
  COUNT(*) AS messages,
  COUNT(read_at) AS read_messages,
  ROUND(AVG((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (read_at - sent_at)) / 86400.0) * 1440), 1) AS avg_read_minutes,
  ROUND(MAX((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (read_at - sent_at)) / 86400.0) * 1440), 1) AS max_read_minutes
FROM messages
WHERE read_at IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY conversation_id
HAVING COUNT(read_at) >= 2
ORDER BY avg_read_minutes DESC;

questions.prompt:

  FROM funnel GROUP BY step

questions.prompt:
Catalog LAD — ASIN with Late-Arriving Metadata


WITH current_dim AS (
  SELECT asin, title, category,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY asin ORDER BY effective_date DESC) AS rn
  FROM asin_dim
)
SELECT o.order_id, o.asin,
  COALESCE(d.title,    'ASIN ' || o.asin || ' (Pending Catalog Sync)') AS title,

FROM orders o

  ON d.asin = o.asin AND d.rn = 1

questions.prompt:
Multi-Warehouse Inventory Routing — Closest-FC Wins

Schema:
```
orders(order_id, customer_zone, ordered_at, customer_id, order_date, status, amount), inventory(asin, warehouse_id, zone, qty_on_hand)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT o.order_id, o.customer_zone, i.warehouse_id, i.zone AS warehouse_zone, i.qty_on_hand,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY o.order_id
      ORDER BY CASE WHEN o.customer_zone = i.zone THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
               i.qty_on_hand DESC
    ) AS pick_order
  FROM orders o
  JOIN inventory i ON i.qty_on_hand > 0
)
SELECT order_id, customer_zone, warehouse_id, warehouse_zone, qty_on_hand
FROM ranked
WHERE pick_order = 1;

questions.prompt:
Sponsored Products Attribution — Click-Through vs View-Through

Schema:
```
ad_impressions(impression_id, user_id, asin, shown_at, clicked), purchases(user_id, asin, purchased_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN i.clicked::text = '1'
       AND EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (p.purchased_at - i.shown_at))/86400.0 BETWEEN 0 AND 7
       THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS click_through_attr,
  SUM(CASE WHEN i.clicked::text = '0'
       AND EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (p.purchased_at - i.shown_at))/86400.0 BETWEEN 0 AND 1
       THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS view_through_attr,
  COUNT(*) AS attributed_pairs
FROM ad_impressions i
JOIN purchases p ON p.user_id = i.user_id AND p.asin = i.asin
  AND p.purchased_at > i.shown_at
  AND (p.purchased_at - i.shown_at) <= INTERVAL '7 days';

  FROM device_purchases
)

    CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (delivered_at - queued_at)) / 86400.0) * 86400 AS INTEGER) AS latency_seconds
  FROM push_events

questions.prompt:
Velocity-Rule Fraud — 3+ Attempts Within 5 Minutes

Schema:
```
payment_attempts(attempt_id, user_id, attempted_at, status)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT a.user_id, a.attempted_at,
  COUNT(b.attempt_id) AS attempts_in_5min
FROM payment_attempts a
JOIN payment_attempts b
  ON b.user_id = a.user_id
 AND julianday(b.attempted_at) BETWEEN julianday(a.attempted_at) - 1.0/288 AND julianday(a.attempted_at)
GROUP BY a.user_id, a.attempted_at

questions.prompt:
MRR Cohort Retention — Monthly Survival Curve

Schema:
```
subscriptions(customer_id, signup_month, active_month, subscription_id, plan, start_date, end_date, status)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH joined AS (
  SELECT signup_month,
    CASE
      WHEN signup_month::text ~ '^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}$' AND active_month::text ~ '^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}$'
        THEN ((EXTRACT(YEAR FROM (active_month||'-01')::date)::int - EXTRACT(YEAR FROM (signup_month||'-01')::date)::int) * 12
              + (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM (active_month||'-01')::date)::int - EXTRACT(MONTH FROM (signup_month||'-01')::date)::int))
      WHEN signup_month::text ~ '^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$' AND active_month::text ~ '^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$'
        THEN ((EXTRACT(YEAR FROM active_month::date)::int - EXTRACT(YEAR FROM signup_month::date)::int) * 12
              + (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM active_month::date)::int - EXTRACT(MONTH FROM signup_month::date)::int))
      ELSE NULL
    END AS month_n,
    customer_id
  FROM subscriptions
)
SELECT signup_month, month_n,
  COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS active_customers,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) /
    FIRST_VALUE(COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id)) OVER (PARTITION BY signup_month ORDER BY month_n), 1) AS retention_pct
FROM joined
WHERE month_n BETWEEN 0 AND 12
GROUP BY signup_month, month_n
ORDER BY signup_month, month_n;

questions.prompt:
Churn Waterfall — New / Existing / Churned / Resurrected

Schema:
```
monthly_revenue(customer_id, month, mrr)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH classified AS (
  SELECT customer_id, month, mrr,
    LAG(mrr) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY month) AS prev_mrr
  FROM monthly_revenue
)
SELECT month,
  SUM(CASE WHEN prev_mrr IS NULL  AND mrr > 0          THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS new_customers,
  SUM(CASE WHEN prev_mrr > 0      AND mrr > 0          THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS existing,
  SUM(CASE WHEN prev_mrr > 0      AND (mrr = 0 OR mrr IS NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS churned,
  SUM(CASE WHEN prev_mrr = 0      AND mrr > 0          THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS resurrected
FROM classified
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;

questions.prompt:
Dispute Rate per Merchant — Risk Scoring

Schema:
```
charges(charge_id, merchant_id, amount, disputed)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT merchant_id,
  COUNT(*) AS charges,
  SUM(CASE WHEN disputed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN disputed::int ELSE 0 END) AS disputes,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN disputed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN disputed::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) AS dispute_rate_pct,
  CASE
    WHEN 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN disputed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN disputed::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) >= 1.0 THEN 'high_risk'
    WHEN 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN disputed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN disputed::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) >= 0.3 THEN 'monitor'
    ELSE 'normal'
  END AS risk_tier
FROM charges
GROUP BY merchant_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5
ORDER BY dispute_rate_pct DESC;

questions.prompt:
Payout Reconciliation — Charges − Refunds = Payout

Schema:
```
charges(charge_id, merchant_id, amount, charged_at), refunds(refund_id, charge_id, amount, refunded_at), payouts(payout_id, merchant_id, amount, paid_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH net AS (
  SELECT c.merchant_id,
    ROUND(SUM(c.amount), 2) AS gross_charges,
    ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(r.amount), 0), 2) AS gross_refunds,
    ROUND(SUM(c.amount) - COALESCE(SUM(r.amount), 0), 2) AS expected_payout
  FROM charges c
  LEFT JOIN refunds r ON r.charge_id = c.charge_id
  GROUP BY c.merchant_id
),
combined AS (
  SELECT n.merchant_id, n.gross_charges, n.gross_refunds, n.expected_payout,
    ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(p.amount), 0), 2) AS actual_payout,
    ROUND(n.expected_payout - COALESCE(SUM(p.amount), 0), 2) AS reconciliation_diff
  FROM net n
  LEFT JOIN payouts p ON p.merchant_id = n.merchant_id
  GROUP BY n.merchant_id, n.gross_charges, n.gross_refunds, n.expected_payout
)
SELECT * FROM combined
ORDER BY ABS(reconciliation_diff) DESC;

questions.prompt:
Occupancy Rate by City — Booked Nights / Available Nights

Schema:
```
calendar_nights(listing_id, night, available, booked, city)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT city,
  COUNT(*) AS listing_nights,
  SUM(CASE WHEN booked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN booked::int ELSE 0 END) AS booked_nights,
  SUM(CASE WHEN available::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN available::int ELSE 0 END) AS available_nights,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN booked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN booked::int ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN available::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN available::int ELSE 0 END)
                      + SUM(CASE WHEN booked::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN booked::int ELSE 0 END), 0), 1) AS occupancy_pct
FROM calendar_nights
GROUP BY city
ORDER BY occupancy_pct DESC;

questions.prompt:
Host Response Time — Median per Listing

Schema:
```
messages(message_id, listing_id, host_id, sent_by, sent_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS messages;
CREATE TABLE messages (
  message_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  listing_id INT,
  host_id INT,
  sent_by TEXT,
  sent_at TIMESTAMP
);
INSERT INTO messages VALUES
  (1, 10, 500, 'guest', '2024-04-01 09:00'),
  (2, 10, 500, 'host',  '2024-04-01 09:10'),
  (3, 10, 500, 'guest', '2024-04-01 10:00'),
  (4, 10, 500, 'host',  '2024-04-01 10:30'),
  (5, 10, 500, 'guest', '2024-04-01 11:00'),
  (6, 10, 500, 'host',  '2024-04-01 11:15'),
  (7, 20, 600, 'guest', '2024-04-02 08:00'),
  (8, 20, 600, 'host',  '2024-04-02 08:05'),
  (9, 20, 600, 'guest', '2024-04-02 09:00'),
  (10,20, 600, 'host',  '2024-04-02 09:20'),
  (11,20, 600, 'guest', '2024-04-02 10:00'),
  (12,20, 600, 'host',  '2024-04-02 10:45'),
  (13,30, 700, 'guest', '2024-04-03 07:00'),
  (14,30, 700, 'host',  '2024-04-03 07:30');

WITH guest_msgs AS (
  SELECT listing_id, sent_at AS guest_at,
    LEAD(sent_at) OVER (PARTITION BY listing_id ORDER BY sent_at) AS host_at,
    LEAD(sent_by) OVER (PARTITION BY listing_id ORDER BY sent_at) AS next_sender
  FROM messages
)
SELECT listing_id,
  COUNT(*) AS guest_messages,
  ROUND(AVG((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (host_at - guest_at)) / 86400.0) * 1440), 1) AS avg_response_minutes
FROM guest_msgs
WHERE next_sender = 'host'
GROUP BY listing_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY avg_response_minutes;

questions.prompt:
Search-to-Book Funnel by Property Type

Schema:
```
search_events(search_id, user_id, property_type, viewed_listing_id, searched_at), bookings(user_id, listing_id, booked_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT s.property_type,
  COUNT(DISTINCT s.search_id) AS searches,
  COUNT(DISTINCT b.user_id) AS booked_users,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT b.user_id) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT s.user_id), 0), 1) AS user_book_pct
FROM search_events s
LEFT JOIN bookings b
  ON b.user_id = s.user_id
 AND b.listing_id = s.viewed_listing_id
 AND EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (b.booked_at - s.searched_at)) / 86400.0 BETWEEN 0 AND 30
GROUP BY s.property_type
ORDER BY user_book_pct DESC;

questions.prompt:
Superhost Eligibility — Multi-Criteria Boolean

Schema:
```
host_metrics(host_id, completed_trips, avg_rating, response_rate_pct, cancellation_count)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT host_id, completed_trips, avg_rating, response_rate_pct, cancellation_count,
  CASE
    WHEN (CASE WHEN completed_trips::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN completed_trips::numeric ELSE 0 END) >= 10
     AND (CASE WHEN avg_rating::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN avg_rating::numeric ELSE 0 END) >= 4.8
     AND (CASE WHEN response_rate_pct::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN response_rate_pct::numeric ELSE 0 END) >= 90
     AND (CASE WHEN cancellation_count::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN cancellation_count::int ELSE 0 END) = 0
    THEN 'superhost' ELSE 'not_eligible'
  END AS status
FROM host_metrics
ORDER BY status, avg_rating DESC;

questions.prompt:
Cancellation Rate by Stay Length

Schema:
```
bookings(booking_id, listing_id, nights, cancelled)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH nb AS (
  SELECT *, CASE WHEN nights::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN nights::int ELSE 0 END AS n_int
  FROM bookings
)
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN n_int <= 2  THEN '1-2 nights'
    WHEN n_int <= 6  THEN '3-6 nights'
    WHEN n_int <= 14 THEN '1-2 weeks'
    ELSE '15+ nights'
  END AS stay_band,
  COUNT(*) AS bookings,
  SUM(CASE WHEN cancelled::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN cancelled::int ELSE 0 END) AS cancellations,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN cancelled::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN cancelled::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS cancel_rate_pct
FROM nb
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY cancel_rate_pct DESC;

questions.prompt:
Calendar Gap Detection — Listings With Black-Out Stretches

Schema:
```
calendar_nights(listing_id, night, available)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH unavail AS (
  SELECT listing_id, night,
    julianday(night) - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY listing_id ORDER BY night) AS island_id
  FROM calendar_nights
  WHERE available = 0
)
SELECT listing_id,
  MIN(night) AS blackout_start,
  MAX(night) AS blackout_end,
  COUNT(*) AS blackout_days
FROM unavail
GROUP BY listing_id, island_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1
ORDER BY blackout_days DESC;

questions.prompt:
Song Skip Rate by Genre

Schema:
```
plays(play_id, user_id, song_id, genre, played_seconds, song_seconds, skipped)
```

questions.model_answer:
SELECT genre,
  COUNT(*) AS plays,
  SUM(CASE WHEN skipped::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN skipped::int ELSE 0 END) AS skips,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN skipped::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN skipped::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS skip_rate_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(played_seconds * 100.0 / NULLIF(song_seconds, 0)), 1) AS avg_completion_pct
FROM plays
GROUP BY genre
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3

questions.prompt:
Playlist Engagement — Listens / Saves / Shares

Schema:
```
playlist_actions(playlist_id, user_id, action_type)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS playlist_actions;
CREATE TABLE playlist_actions (playlist_id INT, user_id INT, action_type TEXT);
INSERT INTO playlist_actions (playlist_id, user_id, action_type) VALUES
  (1, 101, 'listen'), (1, 102, 'listen'), (1, 103, 'listen'),
  (1, 101, 'save'),  (1, 104, 'save'),
  (1, 102, 'share'),
  (2, 201, 'listen'), (2, 202, 'listen'), (2, 203, 'listen'), (2, 204, 'listen'),
  (2, 201, 'save'),
  (2, 205, 'share'), (2, 206, 'share'),
  (3, 301, 'listen'), (3, 302, 'listen'),
  (3, 301, 'save'),
  (4, 401, 'listen'), (4, 402, 'listen'), (4, 403, 'listen'),
  (4, 401, 'save'), (4, 402, 'save'), (4, 403, 'save');
SELECT playlist_id,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'listen' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS listens,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'save'   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS saves,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'share'  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS shares,
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'save' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'listen' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0), 1) AS save_per_listen_pct
FROM playlist_actions

questions.prompt:
Discover Weekly Discovery Rate — Tracks Saved After First Play

Schema:
```
discover_plays(user_id, song_id, played_at), library_saves(user_id, song_id, saved_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.user_id || '|' || d.song_id) AS discovered_pairs,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN s.song_id IS NOT NULL THEN d.user_id || '|' || d.song_id END) AS saved_pairs,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN s.song_id IS NOT NULL THEN d.user_id || '|' || d.song_id END)
              / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT d.user_id || '|' || d.song_id), 0), 1) AS discovery_save_rate_pct
FROM discover_plays d
LEFT JOIN library_saves s
  ON s.user_id = d.user_id
 AND s.song_id = d.song_id
 AND (s.saved_at) >= (d.played_at)
 AND (s.saved_at - d.played_at) <= INTERVAL '7 days';

questions.prompt:
Podcast Episode Completion Rate

Schema:
```
podcast_plays(play_id, episode_id, listened_seconds, episode_seconds)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH banded AS (
  SELECT episode_id,
    CASE
      WHEN listened_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(episode_seconds, 0) >= 0.95 THEN 'completed'
      WHEN listened_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(episode_seconds, 0) >= 0.50 THEN 'engaged'
      WHEN listened_seconds * 1.0 / NULLIF(episode_seconds, 0) >= 0.10 THEN 'sampled'
      ELSE 'bounced'
    END AS engagement_band
  FROM podcast_plays
)
SELECT episode_id, engagement_band, COUNT(*) AS plays
FROM banded
GROUP BY episode_id, engagement_band
ORDER BY episode_id, plays DESC;

  COUNT(*) AS trials,
  COUNT(converted_to_paid) AS converted,

questions.prompt:
Royalty Pool Pro-Rata — Streams × Country Rate

Schema:
```
streams(stream_id, artist_id, country, played_at), country_pool(country, monthly_pool_usd)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH streams_per_artist AS (
  SELECT s.country, s.artist_id, COUNT(*) AS streams
  FROM streams s GROUP BY s.country, s.artist_id
),
country_total AS (
  SELECT country, SUM(streams) AS total_streams
  FROM streams_per_artist GROUP BY country
),
pool_clean AS (
  SELECT country,
    CASE WHEN monthly_pool_usd::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN monthly_pool_usd::numeric ELSE 0 END AS monthly_pool_usd
  FROM country_pool
)
SELECT spa.country, spa.artist_id, spa.streams, ct.total_streams,
  ROUND(spa.streams * 1.0 / NULLIF(ct.total_streams, 0) * cp.monthly_pool_usd, 2) AS payout_usd
FROM streams_per_artist spa
JOIN country_total ct ON ct.country = spa.country
JOIN pool_clean cp ON cp.country = spa.country
ORDER BY payout_usd DESC;

questions.prompt:
Family Plan Member Utilization

Schema:
```
family_plans(account_id, max_members), family_listening(account_id, member_id, listened_minutes)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT fp.account_id, fp.max_members,
  COUNT(DISTINCT fl.member_id) AS active_members,
  ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT fl.member_id) / NULLIF(fp.max_members, 0), 1) AS utilization_pct,
  ROUND(SUM(fl.listened_minutes) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT fl.member_id), 0), 0) AS avg_minutes_per_member
FROM family_plans fp
LEFT JOIN family_listening fl ON fl.account_id = fp.account_id
GROUP BY fp.account_id, fp.max_members
ORDER BY utilization_pct DESC;

questions.prompt:
Audio Quality Bitrate Drops — Per Session

Schema:
```
playback_events(session_id, event_at, bitrate_kbps)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH transitions AS (
  SELECT session_id, event_at, bitrate_kbps,
    LAG(bitrate_kbps) OVER (PARTITION BY session_id ORDER BY event_at) AS prev_bitrate
  FROM playback_events
)
SELECT session_id,
  COUNT(*) AS events,
  SUM(CASE WHEN prev_bitrate IS NOT NULL AND bitrate_kbps != prev_bitrate THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS quality_changes,
  MIN(bitrate_kbps) AS lowest_bitrate,
  MAX(bitrate_kbps) AS highest_bitrate
FROM transitions
GROUP BY session_id
ORDER BY quality_changes DESC;

questions.prompt:
Dasher Utilization — Active Time / Online Time

Schema:
```
dasher_shifts(dasher_id, online_at, offline_at), deliveries(dasher_id, started_at, ended_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT s.dasher_id,
  ROUND(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (s.offline_at - s.online_at)) / 86400.0) * 24.0, 2) AS hours_online,
  ROUND(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.ended_at - d.started_at)) / 86400.0) * 24.0, 2) AS hours_delivering,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.ended_at - d.started_at)) / 86400.0)
              / NULLIF(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (s.offline_at - s.online_at)) / 86400.0), 0), 1) AS utilization_pct
FROM dasher_shifts s
LEFT JOIN deliveries d ON d.dasher_id = s.dasher_id
GROUP BY s.dasher_id
ORDER BY utilization_pct DESC;

questions.prompt:
Delivery Time SLA — Within 30 Minutes

Schema:
```
orders(order_id, ordered_at, delivered_at, market, customer_id, order_date, status, amount)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT market,
  COUNT(*) AS deliveries,
  SUM(CASE WHEN (EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (delivered_at - ordered_at)) / 86400.0) * 1440 <= 30 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS within_30min,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN (EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (delivered_at - ordered_at)) / 86400.0) * 1440 <= 30 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS sla_pct,
  ROUND(AVG((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (delivered_at - ordered_at)) / 86400.0) * 1440), 1) AS avg_minutes
FROM orders
WHERE delivered_at IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY market
ORDER BY sla_pct DESC;

questions.prompt:
Restaurant Performance — Volume × Rating Composite

Schema:
```
restaurants(restaurant_id, name, market, cuisine, city), orders(order_id, restaurant_id, rating, customer_id, order_date, status, amount)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT r.restaurant_id, r.name, r.market,
  COUNT(o.order_id) AS orders,
  ROUND(AVG(o.rating), 2) AS avg_rating,
  ROUND(AVG(o.rating) * 0.7 + (COUNT(o.order_id) * 1.0 / 100) * 0.3, 2) AS composite_score
FROM restaurants r
LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.restaurant_id = r.restaurant_id
GROUP BY r.restaurant_id, r.name, r.market
HAVING COUNT(o.order_id) >= 3
ORDER BY composite_score DESC;

questions.prompt:
Multi-Order Stacking — Two Orders Same Dasher Same Hour

Schema:
```
deliveries(delivery_id, dasher_id, started_at, restaurant_id)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT a.dasher_id, a.delivery_id AS order_a, b.delivery_id AS order_b,
  CAST(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (b.started_at - a.started_at))/60 AS INTEGER) AS minutes_apart
FROM deliveries a
JOIN deliveries b
  ON b.dasher_id = a.dasher_id
 AND b.delivery_id != a.delivery_id
 AND b.started_at > a.started_at
 AND (b.started_at - a.started_at) <= INTERVAL '1 hour'
ORDER BY a.dasher_id, a.started_at;

questions.prompt:
Surge Multiplier Effectiveness — Lift Above Normal Volume

Schema:
```
orders(order_id, market, ordered_at, fare, surge_multiplier, customer_id, order_date, status, amount)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT market,
  CASE
    WHEN surge_multiplier <  1.3 THEN 'normal'
    WHEN surge_multiplier <  1.7 THEN 'mild'
    ELSE 'heavy'
  END AS surge_band,
  COUNT(*) AS orders,
  ROUND(AVG(fare), 2) AS avg_fare,
  ROUND(SUM(fare), 2) AS total_revenue
FROM orders
GROUP BY market, surge_band
ORDER BY market, surge_band;

questions.prompt:
Customer Cohort LTV — 90-Day Spend per Acquisition Month

Schema:
```
orders(customer_id, ordered_at, total, order_id, order_date, status, amount)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH cohorts AS (
  SELECT customer_id, MIN(ordered_at) AS first_order_at
  FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id
),
spend_in_window AS (
  SELECT c.customer_id,
    TO_CHAR(c.first_order_at::date, 'YYYY-MM') AS cohort_month,
    SUM(o.total) AS cumulative_spend
  FROM cohorts c
  JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
    AND (o.ordered_at::date - c.first_order_at::date) BETWEEN 0 AND 90
  GROUP BY c.customer_id, TO_CHAR(c.first_order_at::date, 'YYYY-MM')
)
SELECT cohort_month,
  COUNT(*) AS new_customers,
  ROUND(AVG(cumulative_spend), 2) AS avg_90d_ltv,
  ROUND(SUM(cumulative_spend), 2) AS cohort_total_spend
FROM spend_in_window
GROUP BY cohort_month
ORDER BY cohort_month;

questions.prompt:
Order Rejection Rate by Restaurant Tier

Schema:
```
order_offers(offer_id, restaurant_id, dasher_id, status, tier)
```

  COUNT(*) AS offers,
  SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'rejected' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS rejected,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'rejected' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS rejection_rate_pct
FROM order_offers
GROUP BY tier
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY rejection_rate_pct DESC;

questions.prompt:
Tip Pattern by Order Size + Time of Day

Schema:
```
orders(order_id, total, tip, ordered_at, customer_id, order_date, status, amount)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN total < 25 THEN 'small'
    WHEN total < 60 THEN 'medium'
    ELSE 'large'
  END AS order_band,
  CASE
    WHEN CAST(TO_CHAR(ordered_at, 'HH24') AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 17 AND 21 THEN 'dinner'
    WHEN CAST(TO_CHAR(ordered_at, 'HH24') AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 11 AND 14 THEN 'lunch'
    ELSE 'offpeak'
  END AS daypart,
  COUNT(*) AS orders,
  ROUND(AVG(tip), 2) AS avg_tip,
  ROUND(100.0 * AVG(tip * 1.0 / NULLIF(total, 0)), 1) AS tip_pct_of_total
FROM orders
GROUP BY order_band, daypart
ORDER BY order_band, daypart;

questions.prompt:
Connection-of-Connection — 2-Hop Suggestions

Schema:
```
connections(member_id, connection_id)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT a.member_id AS me, b.connection_id AS suggested,
  COUNT(*) AS shared_connections
FROM connections a
JOIN connections b
  ON a.connection_id = b.member_id
 AND a.member_id != b.connection_id
GROUP BY a.member_id, b.connection_id
ORDER BY shared_connections DESC, me, suggested
LIMIT 30;

questions.prompt:
InMail Response Rate by Recipient Industry

Schema:
```
inmails(inmail_id, sender_id, recipient_id, sent_at, replied), members(member_id, industry)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.

  COUNT(*) AS inmails,
  SUM(CASE WHEN i.replied::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN i.replied::int ELSE 0 END) AS replies,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN i.replied::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN i.replied::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS response_rate_pct

JOIN members m ON m.member_id = i.recipient_id

questions.prompt:
Job Application Funnel — Saved → Applied → Recruiter-Viewed

Schema:
```
job_actions(action_id, member_id, job_id, action_type, action_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH per_pair AS (
  SELECT member_id, job_id,
    SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'saved'   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS saved,
    SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'applied' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS applied,
    SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'recruiter_viewed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS recruiter_viewed
  FROM job_actions
  GROUP BY member_id, job_id
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS member_job_pairs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN saved >= 1   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS saved_step,
  SUM(CASE WHEN applied >= 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS applied_step,
  SUM(CASE WHEN recruiter_viewed >= 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS recruiter_viewed_step,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN applied >= 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
              / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN saved >= 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0), 1) AS save_to_apply_pct
FROM per_pair;

questions.prompt:
Feed Post Virality Score — Reactions / Comments / Reshares

Schema:
```
post_engagements(post_id, author_id, action_type)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT post_id, author_id,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'reaction'  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS reactions,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'comment'   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS comments,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'reshare'   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS reshares,
  SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'reaction'  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1
  + SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'comment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 3
  + SUM(CASE WHEN action_type = 'reshare' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 5 AS virality_score
FROM post_engagements
GROUP BY post_id, author_id
ORDER BY virality_score DESC;

questions.prompt:
Recruiter Daily Search Volume

Schema:
```
recruiter_searches(search_id, recruiter_id, searched_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH per_day AS (
  SELECT recruiter_id, DATE(searched_at) AS day, COUNT(*) AS searches
  FROM recruiter_searches
  GROUP BY recruiter_id, DATE(searched_at)
)
SELECT recruiter_id, day, searches,
  SUM(searches) OVER (PARTITION BY recruiter_id) AS total_searches,
  COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY recruiter_id) AS active_days
FROM per_day

questions.prompt:

SELECT

    WHEN CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ('2024-04-01' - b.created_at)) / 86400.0) / 30 AS INTEGER) <= 1 THEN 'new (<= 1mo)'
    WHEN CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ('2024-04-01' - b.created_at)) / 86400.0) / 30 AS INTEGER) <= 6 THEN '1-6 mo'
    WHEN CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ('2024-04-01' - b.created_at)) / 86400.0) / 30 AS INTEGER) <= 12 THEN '6-12 mo'
    ELSE '12+ mo'
  END AS board_age_band,
  COUNT(DISTINCT b.board_id) AS boards,
  COUNT(bp.pin_id) AS total_pins,
  ROUND(COUNT(bp.pin_id) * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT b.board_id), 0), 1) AS avg_pins_per_board
FROM boards b
LEFT JOIN board_pins bp ON bp.board_id = b.board_id
GROUP BY board_age_band
ORDER BY avg_pins_per_board DESC;

questions.prompt:
Account Hierarchy — Recursive Parent-Child Rollup

Schema:
```
accounts(account_id, name, parent_account_id, arr, plan, signup_date, country)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH RECURSIVE chain AS (
  SELECT account_id, name, parent_account_id, arr, account_id AS root_id, 1 AS depth
  FROM accounts WHERE parent_account_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT a.account_id, a.name, a.parent_account_id, a.arr, c.root_id, c.depth + 1
  FROM accounts a JOIN chain c ON a.parent_account_id = c.account_id
)
SELECT root_id, MAX(depth) AS tree_depth, COUNT(*) AS total_accounts,
  ROUND(SUM(arr), 2) AS rolled_up_arr
FROM chain
GROUP BY root_id
ORDER BY rolled_up_arr DESC;

  FROM opp_activities
  GROUP BY opp_id
)
SELECT opp_id, most_recent,
  CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ('2024-04-01' - most_recent)) / 86400.0) AS INTEGER) AS days_idle,
  CASE
    WHEN ('2024-04-01' - most_recent) > INTERVAL '60 days' THEN 'critical_stale'
    WHEN ('2024-04-01' - most_recent) > INTERVAL '30 days' THEN 'stale'
    ELSE 'active'
  END AS health
FROM last_activity
ORDER BY days_idle DESC;

questions.prompt:
Customer Health Score — Composite

Schema:
```
customer_signals(customer_id, login_30d_count, support_tickets_open, nps, last_renewal_pct)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH s AS (
  SELECT customer_id, login_30d_count, support_tickets_open, nps, last_renewal_pct,
    CASE WHEN login_30d_count::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN login_30d_count::numeric ELSE 0 END AS l_n,
    CASE WHEN support_tickets_open::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN support_tickets_open::numeric ELSE 0 END AS s_t,
    CASE WHEN nps::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN nps::numeric ELSE 0 END AS n_v,
    CASE WHEN last_renewal_pct::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN last_renewal_pct::numeric ELSE 0 END AS lr
  FROM customer_signals
)
SELECT customer_id, login_30d_count, support_tickets_open, nps, last_renewal_pct,
  ROUND(
    LEAST(l_n * 1.0 / 10, 1.0) * 30
    + (CASE WHEN s_t <= 1 THEN 25 WHEN s_t <= 3 THEN 15 ELSE 0 END)
    + (n_v + 100) / 200.0 * 25
    + lr * 0.20
  , 1) AS health_score
FROM s
ORDER BY health_score DESC;

questions.prompt:
Creator Earnings by Tier — Creator Fund


WITH c AS (

    CASE WHEN follower_count::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN follower_count::int ELSE 0 END AS follower_count,
    CASE WHEN total_views::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN total_views::int ELSE 0 END AS total_views,
    CASE WHEN earnings::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN earnings::numeric ELSE 0 END AS earnings

  ROUND(AVG(earnings), 2) AS avg_earnings,

ORDER BY avg_earnings DESC;

questions.prompt:
Hashtag Trend Detection — Velocity Increase

Schema:
```
hashtag_uses(hashtag_id, used_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH weekly AS (
  SELECT hashtag_id,
    CAST((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (used_at - '2024-01-01')) / 86400.0) / 7 AS INTEGER) AS week_n,
    COUNT(*) AS uses
  FROM hashtag_uses
  GROUP BY hashtag_id, week_n
)
SELECT hashtag_id, week_n, uses,
  LAG(uses) OVER (PARTITION BY hashtag_id ORDER BY week_n) AS prev_week,
  ROUND(100.0 * (uses - LAG(uses) OVER (PARTITION BY hashtag_id ORDER BY week_n))
              / NULLIF(LAG(uses) OVER (PARTITION BY hashtag_id ORDER BY week_n), 0), 1) AS wow_pct
FROM weekly
ORDER BY hashtag_id, week_n;

questions.prompt:

```
comments(comment_id, parent_id, video_id, likes)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH RECURSIVE thread AS (
  SELECT comment_id, parent_id, video_id, likes, 1 AS depth
  FROM comments WHERE parent_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT c.comment_id, c.parent_id, c.video_id, c.likes, t.depth + 1
  FROM comments c JOIN thread t ON c.parent_id = t.comment_id
)
SELECT video_id,
  MAX(depth) AS max_thread_depth,
  COUNT(*) AS total_comments,
  SUM(likes) AS total_likes
FROM thread
GROUP BY video_id
ORDER BY max_thread_depth DESC;

  (6, 21, 1, 'duet'),
  (7, 22, 1, 'stitch'),
  (8, 23, 2, 'duet'),
  (9, 24, 2, 'stitch'),
  (10, 25, 2, 'stitch'),
  (11, 26, 3, 'duet');
SELECT v.video_id AS original_video,
  COUNT(d.video_id) AS derivatives,
  SUM(CASE WHEN d.video_type = 'duet'   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS duets,
  SUM(CASE WHEN d.video_type = 'stitch' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS stitches
FROM videos v
LEFT JOIN videos d ON d.parent_video_id = v.video_id
WHERE v.parent_video_id IS NULL
GROUP BY v.video_id
ORDER BY derivatives DESC
LIMIT 20;

questions.prompt:

questions.prompt:
Subreddit Growth — Subscriber WoW Velocity

Schema:
```
subreddit_subs(subreddit_id, week, subscribers)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH s AS (
  SELECT subreddit_id, week,
    CASE WHEN subscribers::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN subscribers::int ELSE 0 END AS subscribers
  FROM subreddit_subs
)
SELECT subreddit_id, week, subscribers,
  LAG(subscribers) OVER (PARTITION BY subreddit_id ORDER BY week) AS prev_week,
  subscribers - LAG(subscribers) OVER (PARTITION BY subreddit_id ORDER BY week) AS net_new,
  ROUND(100.0 * (subscribers - LAG(subscribers) OVER (PARTITION BY subreddit_id ORDER BY week))
              / NULLIF(LAG(subscribers) OVER (PARTITION BY subreddit_id ORDER BY week), 0), 1) AS wow_growth_pct
FROM s
ORDER BY subreddit_id, week;

Moderator Removal Rate by Subreddit

  SUM(CASE WHEN removed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN removed::int ELSE 0 END) AS removed,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN removed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN removed::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS removal_rate_pct,
  CASE
    WHEN 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN removed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN removed::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) >= 30 THEN 'over_moderated'
    WHEN 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN removed::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN removed::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) >= 10 THEN 'active'

  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN auto_resolved::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+$' THEN auto_resolved::int ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS noise_ratio_pct,

    CASE WHEN hosts_quota::text ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN hosts_quota::numeric ELSE 0 END AS hosts_quota

  FROM sales

questions.prompt:
MoM Department Increase — Why LAG Returns NULL

Schema:
```
orders(order_id, customer_id, order_date, order_amount, department_id, status, amount), departments(department_id, department_name, dept_id, location, manager_id)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH months AS (
  SELECT '2024-01' AS order_month UNION ALL SELECT '2024-02'
),
dept_months AS (
  SELECT d.department_id, d.department_name, m.order_month
  FROM departments d CROSS JOIN months m
),
monthly_totals AS (
  SELECT dm.department_id, dm.department_name, dm.order_month,
    COALESCE(SUM(o.order_amount), 0) AS monthly_amount
  FROM dept_months dm
  LEFT JOIN orders o
    ON o.department_id = dm.department_id
   AND TO_CHAR(o.order_date, 'YYYY-MM') = dm.order_month
  GROUP BY dm.department_id, dm.department_name, dm.order_month
),
mom AS (
  SELECT department_id, department_name, order_month,
    monthly_amount - LAG(monthly_amount) OVER (
      PARTITION BY department_id ORDER BY order_month
    ) AS mom_increase
  FROM monthly_totals
)
SELECT department_id, department_name, mom_increase
FROM mom
WHERE order_month = '2024-02'
ORDER BY mom_increase DESC NULLS LAST
LIMIT 1;

questions.prompt:
Order Status Periods — Deduplicating Consecutive Status Pings

Schema:
```
orders(order_id, order_date, status, customer_id, amount)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH status_changes AS (
  SELECT order_id, order_date, status,
    LAG(status) OVER (PARTITION BY order_id ORDER BY order_date) AS prev_status
  FROM orders
),
filtered AS (
  SELECT order_id, order_date, status
  FROM status_changes
  WHERE status != prev_status OR prev_status IS NULL
)
SELECT order_id, status,
  order_date AS start_date,
  LEAD(order_date) OVER (PARTITION BY order_id ORDER BY order_date) AS end_date
FROM filtered
ORDER BY order_id, start_date;

questions.prompt:
Subscription Status Periods — Lifecycle Stitching

Schema:
```
subscription_events(account_id, status, recorded_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH changes AS (
  SELECT account_id, recorded_at, status,
    LAG(status) OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY recorded_at) AS prev_status
  FROM subscription_events
),
filtered AS (
  SELECT account_id, recorded_at, status
  FROM changes
  WHERE status != prev_status OR prev_status IS NULL
)
SELECT account_id, status,
  recorded_at AS period_start,
  LEAD(recorded_at) OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY recorded_at) AS period_end
FROM filtered
ORDER BY account_id, period_start;

questions.prompt:
Driver Online Sessions — Collapsing Status Pings

Schema:
```
driver_status_pings(driver_id, status, ping_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH transitions AS (
  SELECT driver_id, ping_at, status,
    CASE WHEN status != COALESCE(LAG(status) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id ORDER BY ping_at), '__none__')
         THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_change
  FROM driver_status_pings
),
sessioned AS (
  SELECT driver_id, ping_at, status,
    SUM(is_change) OVER (PARTITION BY driver_id ORDER BY ping_at) AS session_id
  FROM transitions
)
SELECT driver_id, session_id, status,
  MIN(ping_at) AS session_start,
  MAX(ping_at) AS session_end,
  COUNT(*)    AS pings_in_session
FROM sessioned
GROUP BY driver_id, session_id, status
ORDER BY driver_id, session_start;

questions.prompt:
Payment State Lifecycle — Terminal-State Closure

Schema:
```
payment_state_log(payment_id, state, changed_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH changes AS (
  SELECT payment_id, changed_at, state,
    LAG(state) OVER (PARTITION BY payment_id ORDER BY changed_at) AS prev_state
  FROM payment_state_log
),
filtered AS (
  SELECT payment_id, changed_at, state
  FROM changes
  WHERE state IS DISTINCT FROM prev_state
)
SELECT payment_id, state,
  changed_at AS state_start,
  LEAD(changed_at) OVER (PARTITION BY payment_id ORDER BY changed_at) AS state_end,
  CASE WHEN state IN ('succeeded','failed','refunded','disputed') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_terminal
FROM filtered
ORDER BY payment_id, state_start;

questions.prompt:
Opportunity Stage Periods — Allowing Backward Transitions

Schema:
```
opp_stage_history(opp_id, stage, stage_rank, entered_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH changes AS (
  SELECT opp_id, entered_at, stage, stage_rank,
    LAG(stage)      OVER (PARTITION BY opp_id ORDER BY entered_at) AS prev_stage,
    LAG(stage_rank) OVER (PARTITION BY opp_id ORDER BY entered_at) AS prev_rank
  FROM opp_stage_history
),
filtered AS (
  SELECT opp_id, entered_at, stage, stage_rank, prev_rank
  FROM changes
  WHERE stage != prev_stage OR prev_stage IS NULL
)
SELECT opp_id, stage,
  entered_at AS period_start,
  LEAD(entered_at) OVER (PARTITION BY opp_id ORDER BY entered_at) AS period_end,
  CASE
    WHEN prev_rank IS NULL                        THEN 'initial'
    WHEN stage_rank > prev_rank                   THEN 'forward'
    WHEN stage_rank < prev_rank                   THEN 'backward'
    ELSE 'lateral'
  END AS direction
FROM filtered
ORDER BY opp_id, period_start;

questions.prompt:
Member Position Periods — Per-Member Per-Company Tenure

Schema:
```
member_company_events(member_id, company_id, event_type, event_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH paired AS (
  SELECT member_id, company_id, event_type, event_at,
    LAG(event_type) OVER (PARTITION BY member_id, company_id ORDER BY event_at) AS prev_event
  FROM member_company_events
),
filtered AS (
  SELECT member_id, company_id, event_type, event_at
  FROM paired
  WHERE event_type != prev_event OR prev_event IS NULL
)
SELECT member_id, company_id,
  MIN(CASE WHEN event_type = 'started'  THEN event_at END) AS started_at,
  MAX(CASE WHEN event_type = 'ended'    THEN event_at END) AS ended_at
FROM filtered
GROUP BY member_id, company_id
ORDER BY member_id, started_at;

questions.prompt:
Order Delivery State Periods — Parallel Restaurant + Dasher States

Schema:
```
order_state_pings(order_id, restaurant_state, dasher_state, ping_at)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH composite AS (
  SELECT order_id, ping_at,
    restaurant_state || '|' || dasher_state AS combined_state,
    restaurant_state, dasher_state
  FROM order_state_pings
),
changes AS (
  SELECT order_id, ping_at, combined_state, restaurant_state, dasher_state,
    LAG(combined_state) OVER (PARTITION BY order_id ORDER BY ping_at) AS prev_combined
  FROM composite
),
filtered AS (
  SELECT order_id, ping_at, restaurant_state, dasher_state, combined_state
  FROM changes
  WHERE combined_state != prev_combined OR prev_combined IS NULL
)
SELECT order_id, restaurant_state, dasher_state,
  ping_at AS period_start,
  LEAD(ping_at) OVER (PARTITION BY order_id ORDER BY ping_at) AS period_end
FROM filtered
ORDER BY order_id, period_start;

questions.prompt:
RFM Tier Segmentation — Multi-Window Recency + Y1 vs Y2 Tier Shift

Snapshot date is 2025-04-01. Compute per repeat customer (>1 paid order):
  • l7_orders, l28_orders, l90_orders   — paid orders in those windows
  • y1_spend, y2_spend                  — rolling-12 vs prior-12
  • yoy_growth_pct                      — (y1 - y2) / y2 × 100
  • current_tier  — Tier 1 if rfm_pct ≥ 0.90 (rfm composed of
                    PERCENT_RANK over recency_days DESC,
                    frequency ASC, monetary ASC); Tier 2 if ≥ 0.75;
                    else Tier 3.
  • prior_tier    — same calculation but using only Y2 paid orders.
  • tier_shift    — 'fell','climbed','stable','new_repeat' (no Y2 data).
Sort by tier_shift = 'fell' first (most urgent), then by yoy_growth_pct ASC.

Schema:
```
orders(order_id, customer_id, order_date, amount, status)
```
questions.model_answer:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS orders;
CREATE TABLE orders (
    order_id    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    customer_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    order_date  DATE    NOT NULL,
    amount      NUMERIC NOT NULL,
    status      TEXT    NOT NULL
);

  (3,1,'2023-11-18',1100,'paid'), (4,1,'2024-01-30',850,'paid'),
  (5,1,'2024-03-14',780,'paid'),  (6,1,'2024-06-22',300,'paid'),
  (7,1,'2024-11-08',180,'paid'),  (8,1,'2025-03-28',150,'paid'),
  (9,2,'2023-06-01',300,'paid'),   (10,2,'2023-10-14',280,'paid'),
  (11,2,'2024-02-22',310,'paid'),  (12,2,'2024-08-10',900,'paid'),
  (13,2,'2024-12-05',1100,'paid'), (14,2,'2025-02-15',1500,'paid'),
  (15,2,'2025-03-25',1300,'paid'),
  (16,3,'2023-07-05',60,'paid'), (17,3,'2024-01-11',55,'paid'),
  (18,3,'2024-09-30',70,'paid'), (19,3,'2025-02-04',65,'paid'),
  (20,4,'2024-09-08',420,'paid'), (21,4,'2024-12-15',520,'paid'),
  (22,4,'2025-03-22',610,'paid'),
  (23,5,'2025-03-30',800,'paid'),
  (24,6,'2025-03-30',500,'refunded'), (25,6,'2024-08-10',180,'paid'),
  (26,6,'2024-02-15',150,'paid');

WITH params AS (SELECT DATE '2025-04-01' AS snapshot),
paid AS (
    SELECT o.customer_id, o.order_date, o.amount,
           (p.snapshot - o.order_date) AS days_back
    FROM orders o, params p
    WHERE o.status = 'paid'
),
agg AS (
    SELECT customer_id,
        MIN(CASE WHEN days_back < 365 THEN days_back END) AS recency_days,
        SUM(CASE WHEN days_back < 365 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS y1_orders,
        ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN days_back < 365 THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 2) AS y1_spend,
        SUM(CASE WHEN days_back >= 365 AND days_back < 730 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS y2_orders,
        ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN days_back >= 365 AND days_back < 730 THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 2) AS y2_spend,
        SUM(CASE WHEN days_back < 7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS l7_orders,
        SUM(CASE WHEN days_back < 28 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS l28_orders,
        SUM(CASE WHEN days_back < 90 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS l90_orders,
        COUNT(*) AS lifetime_orders,
        ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS lifetime_spend
    FROM paid
    GROUP BY customer_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
),
scored_now AS (
    SELECT agg.*,
        PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY recency_days DESC) AS r_pct,
        PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY y1_orders ASC) AS f_pct,
        PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY y1_spend ASC) AS m_pct
    FROM agg WHERE y1_orders > 0
),
scored_prior AS (
    SELECT customer_id, y2_orders, y2_spend,
        PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY y2_orders ASC) AS f2_pct,
        PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY y2_spend ASC) AS m2_pct
    FROM agg WHERE y2_orders > 0
),
labelled AS (
    SELECT n.*,
        ROUND((n.r_pct + n.f_pct + n.m_pct)::numeric / 3.0, 3) AS rfm_pct,
        CASE WHEN (n.r_pct + n.f_pct + n.m_pct)/3.0 >= 0.90 THEN 'Tier 1'
             WHEN (n.r_pct + n.f_pct + n.m_pct)/3.0 >= 0.75 THEN 'Tier 2'
             ELSE 'Tier 3' END AS current_tier,
        ROUND((p.f2_pct + p.m2_pct)::numeric / 2.0, 3) AS prior_pct,
        CASE
            WHEN p.customer_id IS NULL THEN 'new_repeat'
            WHEN (p.f2_pct + p.m2_pct)/2.0 >= 0.75 THEN 'Tier 1'
            WHEN (p.f2_pct + p.m2_pct)/2.0 >= 0.50 THEN 'Tier 2'
            ELSE 'Tier 3' END AS prior_tier
    FROM scored_now n
    LEFT JOIN scored_prior p USING (customer_id)
)
SELECT customer_id,
    l7_orders, l28_orders, l90_orders,
    y1_orders, y1_spend, y2_orders, y2_spend,
    CASE WHEN y2_spend > 0 THEN ROUND(100.0*(y1_spend - y2_spend)/y2_spend, 1) END AS yoy_growth_pct,
    rfm_pct, current_tier, prior_tier,
    CASE
        WHEN prior_tier = 'new_repeat' THEN 'new_repeat'
        WHEN current_tier = prior_tier THEN 'stable'
        WHEN current_tier > prior_tier THEN 'fell'
        ELSE 'climbed'
    END AS tier_shift
FROM labelled
ORDER BY
    CASE WHEN current_tier > prior_tier AND prior_tier <> 'new_repeat' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
    yoy_growth_pct ASC NULLS LAST;

questions.prompt:
Long-Standing Loyal Customers — Y1 vs Y2, Quarterly Mix, Longest Active Streak

Snapshot 2025-04-01. Return one row per customer who passes:
  • tenure_days   ≥ 730 (two full years between first and last order)
  • months_active ≥ 12
  • longest_streak ≥ 6 (six consecutive active months)

Columns:
  customer_id, first_order_date, last_order_date, months_active,
  longest_streak, max_idle_months, lifetime_orders, lifetime_spend,
  y1_spend, y2_spend, yoy_growth_pct,
  q1_spend, q2_spend, q3_spend, q4_spend,           -- of the most recent year
  l28_spend, prior_l28_spend, l28_velocity,         -- ratio L28 / prior-L28
  loyalty_rank                                      -- DENSE_RANK by y1_spend

Sort by y1_spend DESC, yoy_growth_pct DESC.

Schema:
```
orders(order_id, customer_id, order_date, amount, status)
```
questions.model_answer:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS orders;
CREATE TABLE orders (
    order_id    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    customer_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    order_date  DATE    NOT NULL,
    amount      NUMERIC NOT NULL
);
INSERT INTO orders VALUES
  (1,1,'2023-03-15',180), (2,1,'2023-04-12',160), (3,1,'2023-05-09',170),
  (4,1,'2023-06-18',150), (5,1,'2023-07-22',180), (6,1,'2023-08-25',160),
  (7,1,'2023-09-12',170), (8,1,'2023-10-08',180), (9,1,'2023-11-19',200),
  (10,1,'2023-12-15',220),(11,1,'2024-01-20',180),(12,1,'2024-02-14',170),
  (13,1,'2024-03-09',160),(14,1,'2024-04-22',150),
  (15,1,'2024-07-04',120),(16,1,'2024-09-15',100),(17,1,'2024-12-22',90),
  (18,1,'2025-02-05',80),(19,1,'2025-03-25',75),
  (20,2,'2023-05-01',100),(21,2,'2023-09-15',120),
  (22,2,'2024-08-15',110),(23,2,'2025-03-15',130),
  (24,3,'2023-04-12',60),(25,3,'2023-05-19',70),(26,3,'2023-06-21',75),
  (27,3,'2023-07-14',80),(28,3,'2023-08-22',85),(29,3,'2023-09-30',90),
  (30,3,'2023-10-18',95),(31,3,'2023-11-25',100),
  (32,3,'2024-01-10',110),(33,3,'2024-02-22',120),(34,3,'2024-03-15',130),
  (35,3,'2024-04-20',150),(36,3,'2024-05-30',180),(37,3,'2024-06-25',200),
  (38,3,'2024-07-15',220),(39,3,'2024-08-19',240),(40,3,'2024-09-22',280),
  (41,3,'2024-10-30',300),(42,3,'2024-11-14',320),(43,3,'2024-12-18',360),
  (44,3,'2025-01-22',400),(45,3,'2025-02-15',450),(46,3,'2025-03-28',520);

WITH params AS (SELECT DATE '2025-04-01' AS snapshot),
e AS (
    SELECT o.customer_id, o.order_date, o.amount,
           TO_CHAR(o.order_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS ym,
           (p.snapshot - o.order_date) AS days_back
    FROM orders o, params p
),
monthly AS (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id, ym FROM e),
indexed AS (
    SELECT customer_id, ym,
           (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_DATE(ym||'-01','YYYY-MM-DD'))::INT - 2000) * 12
             + EXTRACT(MONTH FROM TO_DATE(ym||'-01','YYYY-MM-DD'))::INT AS m_idx,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY ym) AS rn
    FROM monthly
),
islands AS (
    SELECT customer_id, (m_idx - rn) AS island_id, COUNT(*) AS streak_len
    FROM indexed
    GROUP BY customer_id, (m_idx - rn)
),
streak_per_cust AS (
    SELECT customer_id, MAX(streak_len) AS longest_streak
    FROM islands GROUP BY customer_id
),
idle_gaps AS (
    SELECT customer_id,
           m_idx - LAG(m_idx) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY m_idx) - 1 AS idle_months
    FROM indexed
),
idle_per_cust AS (
    SELECT customer_id, COALESCE(MAX(idle_months), 0) AS max_idle_months
    FROM idle_gaps GROUP BY customer_id
),
agg AS (
    SELECT e.customer_id,
           MIN(order_date) AS first_order_date,
           MAX(order_date) AS last_order_date,
           COUNT(DISTINCT ym) AS months_active,
           COUNT(*) AS lifetime_orders,
           ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS lifetime_spend,
           (MAX(order_date) - MIN(order_date)) AS tenure_days,
           ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN days_back < 365 THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 2) AS y1_spend,
           ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN days_back >= 365 AND days_back < 730 THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 2) AS y2_spend,
           ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN days_back < 90 THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 2) AS q1_spend,
           ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN days_back >= 90 AND days_back < 180 THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 2) AS q2_spend,
           ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN days_back >= 180 AND days_back < 270 THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 2) AS q3_spend,
           ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN days_back >= 270 AND days_back < 365 THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 2) AS q4_spend,
           ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN days_back < 28 THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 2) AS l28_spend,
           ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN days_back >= 28 AND days_back < 56 THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 2) AS prior_l28_spend
    FROM e GROUP BY e.customer_id
),
joined AS (
    SELECT a.*, sp.longest_streak, ip.max_idle_months
    FROM agg a
    JOIN streak_per_cust sp ON sp.customer_id = a.customer_id
    JOIN idle_per_cust   ip ON ip.customer_id = a.customer_id
)
SELECT customer_id, first_order_date, last_order_date,
       months_active, longest_streak, max_idle_months,
       lifetime_orders, lifetime_spend, y1_spend, y2_spend,
       CASE WHEN y2_spend > 0 THEN ROUND(100.0*(y1_spend - y2_spend)/y2_spend, 1) END AS yoy_growth_pct,
       q1_spend, q2_spend, q3_spend, q4_spend,
       l28_spend, prior_l28_spend,
       CASE WHEN prior_l28_spend > 0 THEN ROUND(l28_spend/prior_l28_spend, 2) END AS l28_velocity,
       DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY y1_spend DESC) AS loyalty_rank
FROM joined
WHERE tenure_days >= 730 AND months_active >= 12 AND longest_streak >= 6
ORDER BY y1_spend DESC, yoy_growth_pct DESC;

questions.prompt:
Customer Growth Accounting — New, Resurrected, Existing, Churned

Produce one row per (year_month, bucket) with:
  year_month  | bucket       | customers
where bucket ∈ ('new', 'resurrected', 'existing', 'churned'). Customers count is positive for new/resurrected/existing, and we report churned as a separate positive number (the consumer can negate it).

Schema:
```
orders(order_id, customer_id, order_date, amount, status)
```
questions.model_answer:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS orders;
CREATE TABLE orders (
    order_id    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    customer_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    order_date  TEXT    NOT NULL,
    amount      REAL    NOT NULL
);
INSERT INTO orders VALUES
  (1, 1, '2025-01-05', 100), (2, 1, '2025-02-08', 100),
  (3, 1, '2025-03-12', 100),
  (4, 2, '2025-01-20',  80), (5, 2, '2025-03-15',  80),
  (6, 3, '2025-02-18',  50),
  (7, 4, '2025-03-22', 200);

-- ── PERF rewrite ─────────────────────────────────────────
--   1. GROUP BY user_id, ym instead of SELECT DISTINCT — early-
--      aggregation pushdown in MPP optimisers. Critical when orders
