```
employees(emp_id, name, salary, department, email, hire_date, dept_id, manager_id, job_title)
```
questions.model_answer:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS employees;
CREATE TABLE employees (
    emp_id      INT PRIMARY KEY,
    name        TEXT NOT NULL,
    salary      NUMERIC NOT NULL,
    department  TEXT NOT NULL
);
INSERT INTO employees VALUES
    (1, 'Alex',   220000, 'Engineering'),
    (2, 'Bailey', 195000, 'Engineering'),
    (3, 'Casey',  180000, 'Engineering'),
    (4, 'Drew',   170000, 'Sales'),
    (5, 'Ellis',  150000, 'Sales'),
    (6, 'Finn',   140000, 'Marketing'),
    (7, 'Gray',   130000, 'Marketing'),
    (8, 'Harper', 125000, 'HR'),
    (9, 'Indy',   115000, 'HR');

-- ╭───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
-- │  THE JSON-AGGREGATION ANSWER (the only truly schema-stable form)  │
-- │   Outer columns:  salary_position, by_department                  │
-- │   Inner keys:     evolve with the data — no DDL when 'Legal'      │
-- │                   appears tomorrow                                │
-- │   Portable:       Postgres jsonb_object_agg                       │
-- │                   Snowflake OBJECT_AGG                            │
-- │                   MySQL 8 JSON_OBJECTAGG                          │
-- │                   BigQuery TO_JSON_STRING(ARRAY_AGG(...))         │
-- ╰───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
WITH RankedSalaries AS (
    SELECT department, salary,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) AS rnk
    FROM employees
)
SELECT
    CASE WHEN rnk = 1 THEN '1st Highest'
         WHEN rnk = 2 THEN '2nd Highest' END                    AS salary_position,
    jsonb_object_agg(department, salary ORDER BY department)    AS by_department
FROM RankedSalaries
WHERE rnk IN (1, 2)
GROUP BY rnk
ORDER BY rnk;

questions.prompt:


questions.prompt:
Summarize Rental Income per Landlord

Schema:
```
landlords(landlord_id, name), properties(property_id, landlord_id), leases(lease_id, property_id, start_date, end_date), payments(payment_id, lease_id, amount, paid_on, customer_id, payment_date, method, status)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.

SELECT l.landlord_id, l.name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT p.property_id)         AS properties,
       COUNT(DISTINCT le.lease_id)           AS active_leases,
       COALESCE(SUM(pay.amount), 0)          AS total_income,
       COALESCE(AVG(pay.amount), 0)          AS avg_payment
FROM landlords  l

ORDER BY total_income DESC;

questions.prompt:
Anonymize User PII Data for a Social Media Platform

```
users(user_id, email, phone, first_name, last_name, dob, name, signup_date, country)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.

SELECT
  user_id,
  MD5(email) AS email_hash,
  REGEXP_REPLACE(phone, '\d(?=\d{4})', '*', 'g')           AS phone_masked,
  LEFT(first_name, 1) || REPEAT('*', LENGTH(first_name) - 1)  AS first_masked,
  LEFT(last_name, 1)  || REPEAT('*', LENGTH(last_name)  - 1)  AS last_masked,

FROM users;

questions.prompt:
Product Sales and Inventory Data

Schema:
```

```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.

SELECT p.product_id, p.name,
       COALESCE(SUM(s.qty), 0) AS units_sold,
       MAX(NULLIF(regexp_replace(i.on_hand::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric) AS current_stock,
       MAX(NULLIF(regexp_replace(i.reorder_point::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric) AS reorder_point,
       CASE WHEN MAX(NULLIF(regexp_replace(i.on_hand::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric) <= MAX(NULLIF(regexp_replace(i.reorder_point::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric) THEN 'REORDER'
            ELSE 'OK' END AS stock_status
FROM products  p
LEFT JOIN sales     s ON p.product_id = s.product_id
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON p.product_id = i.product_id
GROUP BY p.product_id, p.name;

questions.prompt:
Merge Customer Records from Two Sources

Schema:
```
customers(customer_id, name, email, updated_at, signup_date, country, city)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
-- Setup — target customers + staging_customers (incoming changes)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS customers CASCADE;
CREATE TABLE customers (
  customer_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT, email TEXT, updated_at TIMESTAMP
);
INSERT INTO customers VALUES
  (1,'Alice','alice@old.com','2025-01-01 09:00'),
  (2,'Bob',  'bob@old.com',  '2025-01-15 09:00'),
  (3,'Carol','carol@old.com','2025-02-01 09:00');

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS staging_customers CASCADE;
CREATE TABLE staging_customers (
  customer_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT, email TEXT, updated_at TIMESTAMP
);
INSERT INTO staging_customers VALUES
  (2,'Bobby',     'bob@new.com',     '2025-04-01 12:00'),  -- update — newer
  (3,'Carol',     'carol@old.com',   '2024-12-01 09:00'),  -- update — older (skip)
  (4,'Dave',      'dave@new.com',    '2025-04-01 12:00');  -- insert

-- ╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
-- │  Postgres MERGE-equivalent: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT        │
-- │  customer_id is the conflict key; updated_at gates the    │
-- │  UPDATE so older staging rows don't overwrite.            │
-- ╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
INSERT INTO customers (customer_id, name, email, updated_at)
SELECT customer_id, name, email, updated_at FROM staging_customers
ON CONFLICT (customer_id) DO UPDATE
   SET name       = EXCLUDED.name,
       email      = EXCLUDED.email,
       updated_at = EXCLUDED.updated_at
 WHERE EXCLUDED.updated_at > customers.updated_at;

SELECT customer_id, name, email, updated_at FROM customers ORDER BY customer_id;

questions.prompt:
Project Duration, Employees, and Equipment Costs

Schema:
```

```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT p.project_id, p.name,
       (p.end_date::date - p.start_date::date) AS duration_days,
       COUNT(DISTINCT a.emp_id) AS employees,
       COALESCE(SUM(a.hours::numeric * a.hourly_rate::numeric), 0) AS labor_cost,
       COALESCE(SUM(DISTINCT e.cost::numeric), 0) AS equipment_cost,
       COALESCE(SUM(a.hours::numeric * a.hourly_rate::numeric), 0)
         + COALESCE(SUM(DISTINCT e.cost::numeric), 0) AS total_cost
FROM projects p
LEFT JOIN assignments      a ON p.project_id = a.project_id
LEFT JOIN equipment_costs  e ON p.project_id = e.project_id
GROUP BY p.project_id, p.name, p.start_date, p.end_date
ORDER BY total_cost DESC;

       COUNT(*) AS active_subs,
       SUM(p.monthly_price) AS mrr
FROM subscriptions s

SELECT model_type,
       COUNT(*) AS calls,
       AVG(CASE WHEN predicted = actual THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS accuracy,
       AVG(NULLIF(regexp_replace(latency_ms::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric) AS avg_latency_ms,
       PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY NULLIF(regexp_replace(latency_ms::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric) AS p95_latency_ms
FROM model_predictions

questions.prompt:
Customer Churn Across Three Sources


Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH all_churn AS (
  SELECT customer_id, churned_on, 'A' AS src FROM source_a
  UNION ALL
  SELECT customer_id, churned_on, 'B'         FROM source_b
  UNION ALL
  SELECT customer_id, churned_on, 'C'         FROM source_c
),
deduped AS (
  SELECT customer_id, churned_on, src,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY churned_on) AS rn
  FROM all_churn
)
SELECT customer_id, churned_on AS first_churn_date, src AS first_seen_in
FROM deduped
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY first_churn_date;

       a.principal::numeric
         * (r.rate_pct::numeric / 100 / 12)
         / (1 - POWER(1 + r.rate_pct::numeric / 100 / 12, -a.term_months::int)) AS monthly_payment

questions.prompt:
Department Budget Variance

Schema:
```
budgets(dept_id, fiscal_year, budgeted), expenses(expense_id, dept_id, fiscal_year, amount)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT b.dept_id, b.fiscal_year,
       b.budgeted::numeric AS budgeted,
       COALESCE(SUM(e.amount::numeric), 0) AS actual,
       b.budgeted::numeric - COALESCE(SUM(e.amount::numeric), 0) AS variance,
       CASE WHEN b.budgeted::numeric = 0 THEN NULL
            ELSE 100.0 * (COALESCE(SUM(e.amount::numeric), 0) - b.budgeted::numeric) / b.budgeted::numeric
       END AS variance_pct
FROM budgets b
LEFT JOIN expenses e
       ON b.dept_id     = e.dept_id
      AND b.fiscal_year = e.fiscal_year
GROUP BY b.dept_id, b.fiscal_year, b.budgeted
ORDER BY ABS(b.budgeted::numeric - COALESCE(SUM(e.amount::numeric), 0)) DESC;

questions.prompt:
Clean Transactional Data

Schema:
```
transactions(txn_id, account_id, amount, txn_ts, status, ranked, transaction_id, customer_id, transaction_time, txn_type)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT *,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY txn_id ORDER BY txn_ts DESC) AS rn
  FROM transactions
  WHERE status NOT IN ('reversed', 'failed')
),
deduped AS (
  SELECT txn_id, account_id, amount, txn_ts, status,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY account_id, amount, txn_ts ORDER BY txn_id) AS rn2
  FROM ranked
  WHERE rn = 1
)
SELECT txn_id, account_id, amount, txn_ts, status
FROM deduped
WHERE rn2 = 1;

questions.prompt:

```
orders(order_id, order_ts, amount, customer_id, order_date, status)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT order_id, order_ts, amount,
       TO_CHAR(order_ts::timestamp, 'Day') AS day_name,
       CASE WHEN EXTRACT(DOW FROM order_ts::timestamp) IN (0, 6) THEN 'weekend' ELSE 'weekday' END AS day_type
FROM orders
WHERE EXTRACT(DOW FROM order_ts::timestamp) IN (0, 6)
ORDER BY order_ts;

questions.prompt:
Amusement Park Rating Anomalies

Schema:
```
ratings(rating_id, ride_id, user_id, rating, rated_at, stats)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
WITH stats AS (
  SELECT ride_id,
         AVG(rating::numeric) AS mu,
         STDDEV(rating::numeric) AS sd
  FROM ratings
  GROUP BY ride_id
)
SELECT r.rating_id, r.ride_id, r.user_id, r.rating, s.mu, s.sd,
       (r.rating::numeric - s.mu) / NULLIF(s.sd, 0) AS z_score
FROM ratings r
JOIN stats s USING (ride_id)
WHERE s.sd IS NOT NULL AND ABS(r.rating::numeric - s.mu) > 2 * s.sd
ORDER BY ABS((r.rating::numeric - s.mu) / NULLIF(s.sd, 0)) DESC;

       STRING_AGG(t.tag, ', ' ORDER BY t.tag) AS tags
FROM equipment   e
JOIN categories  c ON e.category_id = c.category_id
LEFT JOIN tags   t ON e.equipment_id = t.equipment_id
GROUP BY e.equipment_id, e.name, c.label;

questions.prompt:
Daily Category Sales

Schema:
```
orders(order_id, order_ts, customer_id, order_date, status, amount), order_items(order_id, product_id, qty, price), products(product_id, category, name, price, sku, manufacturer)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT DATE(o.order_ts) AS day, p.category,
       SUM(oi.qty * oi.price) AS revenue
FROM orders       o
JOIN order_items  oi ON o.order_id    = oi.order_id
JOIN products     p  ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
GROUP BY DATE(o.order_ts), p.category
ORDER BY day, revenue DESC;

questions.prompt:
Filter Funded Startups

Schema:
```
funding_rounds(round_id, company_id, amount, round_type, raised_on), companies(company_id, name, industry, country, founded_year)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS funding_rounds;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS companies;
CREATE TABLE companies (
  company_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT
);
CREATE TABLE funding_rounds (
  round_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  company_id INT,
  amount NUMERIC(14,2),
  round_type TEXT,
  raised_on DATE
);
INSERT INTO companies VALUES
  (1, 'Acme Corp'),
  (2, 'Beta Labs'),
  (3, 'Gamma Tech'),
  (4, 'Delta Systems'),
  (5, 'Epsilon Industries');
INSERT INTO funding_rounds VALUES
  (101, 1, 500000,  'Seed',     '2023-01-15'),
  (102, 1, 600000,  'Series A', '2023-08-01'),
  (103, 2, 300000,  'Seed',     '2023-02-10'),
  (104, 2, 400000,  'Series A', '2023-09-12'),
  (105, 2, 250000,  'Series B', '2024-01-05'),
  (106, 3, 1500000, 'Series A', '2023-11-20'),
  (107, 4, 200000,  'Seed',     '2023-03-01'),
  (108, 5, 3000000, 'Series A', '2023-05-15'),
  (109, 5, 2000000, 'Series B', '2024-02-10');

SELECT c.company_id, c.name,
       SUM(f.amount) AS total_raised,
       COUNT(*)      AS rounds
FROM companies      c
JOIN funding_rounds f ON c.company_id = f.company_id
GROUP BY c.company_id, c.name
HAVING SUM(f.amount) >= 1000000
ORDER BY total_raised DESC;

questions.prompt:
Rank Equipment Maintenance

Schema:
```
maintenance(maint_id, equipment_id, maint_date, cost), equipment(equipment_id, name, location)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT e.equipment_id, e.name, e.location,
       COUNT(m.maint_id)  AS maintenance_events,
       SUM(m.cost)        AS total_cost,
       MAX(m.maint_date)  AS last_maintenance,
       RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY e.location ORDER BY SUM(m.cost) DESC) AS cost_rank_in_location,
       DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(m.maint_id) DESC)             AS frequency_rank_global
FROM equipment    e
LEFT JOIN maintenance m ON e.equipment_id = m.equipment_id
GROUP BY e.equipment_id, e.name, e.location
ORDER BY total_cost DESC;

Detect Self Interactions

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.

Average Height Per Floor

Schema:
```
rooms(room_id, building_id, floor, ceiling_height_m)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT building_id, floor,
       AVG(NULLIF(regexp_replace(ceiling_height_m::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric) AS avg_height_m,
       CASE WHEN AVG(NULLIF(regexp_replace(ceiling_height_m::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric) >= 3 THEN 'High'
            WHEN AVG(NULLIF(regexp_replace(ceiling_height_m::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric) >= 2.5 THEN 'Standard'
            ELSE 'Low' END AS height_class
FROM rooms
GROUP BY building_id, floor
ORDER BY building_id, floor;


       COUNT(DISTINCT s.species_id) AS species_count,
       STRING_AGG(s.name, ', ' ORDER BY s.name) AS species_list
FROM species s

ORDER BY species_count DESC;

questions.prompt:
Top Reptile Observations

Schema:
```
observations(obs_id, species_id, observed_at, location_id), species(species_id, common_name, class)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT location_id, species_id, common_name, observation_count
FROM (
  SELECT o.location_id, o.species_id, s.common_name,
         COUNT(*) AS observation_count,
         RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY o.location_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rk
  FROM observations o
  JOIN species      s ON o.species_id = s.species_id
  WHERE s.class = 'Reptilia'
  GROUP BY o.location_id, o.species_id, s.common_name
)
WHERE rk <= 5
ORDER BY location_id, rk;

Daily PE Portfolio Value


Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT h.portfolio_id, h.as_of_date AS day,
       SUM(NULLIF(regexp_replace(h.shares::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric * p.close_price) AS portfolio_value
FROM holdings h
JOIN prices p
  ON h.asset_id   = p.asset_id
 AND h.as_of_date = p.price_date
GROUP BY h.portfolio_id, h.as_of_date
ORDER BY h.portfolio_id, day;

questions.prompt:
Annual GDP Growth Rate

Schema:
```
gdp(country, year, gdp_value)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT country, year, gdp_value,
       LAG(gdp_value) OVER (PARTITION BY country ORDER BY year) AS prev_gdp,
       100.0 * (gdp_value - LAG(gdp_value) OVER (PARTITION BY country ORDER BY year))
             / NULLIF(LAG(gdp_value) OVER (PARTITION BY country ORDER BY year), 0) AS yoy_growth_pct,
       AVG(gdp_value) OVER (PARTITION BY country ORDER BY year
                            ROWS BETWEEN 4 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)              AS rolling_5yr_avg
FROM gdp
ORDER BY country, year;

       NULLIF(regexp_replace(j.sqft::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric * NULLIF(regexp_replace(m.cost_per_sqft::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric AS total_cost

  SELECT a.code, a.name, c.country_name, LENGTH(a.name) AS name_length,
         RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.country_name ORDER BY LENGTH(a.name) DESC) AS rk

  JOIN countries c ON a.country_id = c.country_id

questions.prompt:
Math Expressions

Schema:
```
math_inputs(input_id, expression)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT input_id, expression,
       (SELECT array_agg(m[1]) FROM regexp_matches(expression, '(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)', 'g') m) AS numbers,
       (SELECT array_agg(m[1]) FROM regexp_matches(expression, '([+\-*/])', 'g') m) AS operators,
       (SELECT array_agg(m[1]) FROM regexp_matches(expression, '([()])', 'g') m) AS parentheses,
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM regexp_matches(expression, '\d+(?:\.\d+)?', 'g')) AS number_count,
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM regexp_matches(expression, '[+\-*/]', 'g')) AS operator_count
FROM math_inputs;

questions.prompt:
Stars and Planets

Schema:
```
stars(star_id, name, spectral_type), planets(planet_id, star_id, name, mass_earth, orbital_period_days)
```

Write the SQL query for the task above, then self-rate against the model answer.
questions.model_answer:
SELECT s.name AS star, s.spectral_type,
       COUNT(p.planet_id) AS planet_count,
       AVG(NULLIF(regexp_replace(p.mass_earth::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric) AS avg_planet_mass,
       MIN(NULLIF(regexp_replace(p.orbital_period_days::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric) AS shortest_orbit_days,
       MAX(NULLIF(regexp_replace(p.orbital_period_days::text, '[^-.0-9]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric) AS longest_orbit_days
FROM stars s
LEFT JOIN planets p ON s.star_id = p.star_id
GROUP BY s.name, s.spectral_type
ORDER BY planet_count DESC;
